title: ReAct: Synergizing Reasoning and Acting in Language Models
summary: Introduces the ReAct paradigm interleaving chain-of-thought reasoning with tool actions, enabling LLMs to solve complex tasks by alternating thought and observation steps.

title: Toolformer: Language Models Can Teach Themselves to Use Tools
summary: Demonstrates that LLMs can learn to call external APIs by self-supervised fine-tuning on API call annotations, achieving strong tool-use without explicit prompting.

title: AgentBench: Evaluating LLMs as Agents
summary: Proposes a multi-dimensional benchmark for evaluating LLM agents across 8 environments including web, database, and operating system tasks.

title: Gorilla: Large Language Model Connected with Massive APIs
summary: Trains an LLM to generate accurate API calls from natural language, reducing hallucination via retrieval-aware fine-tuning on API documentation.

title: ToolLLM: Facilitating Large Language Models to Master 16000+ Real-world APIs
summary: Constructs a large-scale tool-use benchmark with 16K+ real APIs and proposes ToolBench for evaluating tool selection and composition capabilities.

title: Augmented Language Models: a Survey
summary: Comprehensive survey of approaches augmenting language models with reasoning, tools, and actions, categorizing methods by the type of external resource used.

title: Identifying the Risks of LM Agents with an LM-Emulated Sandbox
summary: Proposes an LM-emulated sandbox for systematically identifying risks of LLM agents by simulating tool environments and measuring harmful action rates.

title: The Rise and Potential of Large Language Model Based Agents: A Survey
summary: Surveys the landscape of LLM-based agents covering architecture, capabilities, and applications across single-agent and multi-agent settings.

title: Judging LLM-as-a-Judge with MT-Bench and Chatbot Arena
summary: Introduces MT-Bench and Chatbot Arena as complementary benchmarks for evaluating LLM judges, analyzing agreement rates and position biases.

title: A Survey on Large Language Model based Autonomous Agents
summary: Surveys autonomous agents built on LLMs, covering architecture design (profile, memory, planning, action) and evaluation methodologies.

title: WebArena: A Realistic Web Environment for Building Autonomous Agents
summary: Presents a realistic web environment benchmark with self-hosted websites for evaluating autonomous web agents on complex information-seeking and task-completion objectives.

title: SWE-bench: Can Language Models Resolve Real-World GitHub Issues?
summary: Introduces a benchmark of 2294 real-world GitHub issues for evaluating LLM agents on end-to-end software engineering tasks including code editing and test validation.
